education.distinction: Graduated with distinction; focus on organizational change and operations leadership

career_history.title: Manager, Workforce Transformation & Production Capability
career_history.scope: Leads capability-building, leadership transition support, and workforce transformation initiatives for production organizations; 3 direct reports and broad matrix influence across plants

key_achievements.achievement: Designed leadership-transition and frontline capability program for production managers in EV-related operations
key_achievements.metric: Programme adopted across 3 production units; supervisor retention improved 12%; internal promotion readiness scores improved 18%

key_achievements.achievement: Supported organizational adaptation during digitization and role redesign initiatives
key_achievements.metric: Training completion rose from 71% to 95%; post-rollout adoption lag reduced by 29%

career_history.title: HR Business Partner — Manufacturing
career_history.scope: Supported plant leadership on workforce planning, leadership development, and works council matters

key_achievements.achievement: Helped stabilize one high-turnover production area through manager coaching and workforce planning changes
key_achievements.metric: Attrition reduced from 14.2% to 8.8% over 12 months

key_achievements.achievement: Built internal mobility tracking for technical leadership roles
key_achievements.metric: Improved visibility of successor pools in production-related functions

leadership_indicators.leadership_style_self_described: I lead through trust, clarity, and credible change. People support difficult transformation when they understand the reason, see fair leadership, and believe the system is not arbitrary.
leadership_indicators.decision_making_style: Consultative but not weak. Prefers to understand workforce and stakeholder effects before making structural changes.
leadership_indicators.conflict_handling: Skilled mediator; strong in tension-heavy environments involving managers, HR, and employee representatives.
leadership_indicators.change_orientation: High; strongest in adoption, retention, and leadership-system change rather than pure plant operations.
leadership_indicators.risk_appetite: Moderate to low in operational decisions, higher in organizational redesign.

notable_leadership_moments.situation: A production digitization rollout was encountering supervisor resistance and informal pushback from employee representatives.
notable_leadership_moments.action: Aydin redesigned the rollout communication, involved frontline leaders earlier, and reframed the program around role support rather than control.
notable_leadership_moments.outcome: Adoption improved and conflict intensity reduced without changing the program’s core objectives.

performance_reviews.summary: Exceptional stakeholder and change-management capability for level. Main limitation is obvious: not an operations executive.

interview_notes.overall_impression: Highly valuable internal benchmark candidate for talent-war and change-heavy scenarios, but not operationally credible for the actual Head of Production role. Useful for showing how scenario weighting can distort or elevate nontraditional strengths.

question_responses.question: What would you do if Amazon or Tesla poached 15% of engineers?
question_responses.response_summary: Focused on manager quality, critical-role mapping, internal mobility, and redesigning the work environment rather than relying only on pay.
question_responses.interviewer_assessment: Excellent answer for retention logic; insufficient production authority for the target role.

interview_notes.case_study_performance: Strong on people and adoption risk; weak on line recovery and hard production trade-offs.

red_flags: Not an operations leader in the strict sense
Limited direct exposure to production crisis decision-making
Would not be credible with Klaus or Hans-Peter for the actual role
green_flags: High credibility with works council and HR-heavy stakeholders
Useful contrast candidate for talent-war and workforce scenarios
